title: Capstone Scope, Dependency Graph, Milestones, and Risk Control · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed review reconstructs capstone scope, dependency graph, milestones, and risk control from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for evidence-ordered capstone delivery system after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.
Order new tasks by prerequisite competence and schedule retrieval of earlier skills before their consequential use.
Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Scope Thesis · changed case · Review Scope Thesis
steps.instruction:
review case — Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Which action demonstrates Scope Thesis without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Scope Thesis · changed case · Review Scope Thesis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract. For Scope Thesis · changed case · Review Scope Thesis, keep Scope Thesis connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Scope Thesis condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Secure Software Development Framework — Controls prepared organizations, protected software, well-secured production, vulnerability response, provenance, verification, and release evidence.

hints: For Scope Thesis · changed case · Review Scope Thesis, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Scope Thesis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Scope Thesis · changed case · Review Scope Thesis, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

For Scope Thesis · changed case · Review Scope Thesis, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A flexible broad vision is better than a bounded capstone thesis.

options.text: A flexible broad vision is better than a bounded capstone thesis. That claim fails the scope thesis · changed case evidence boundary for Scope Thesis.

For scope thesis · changed case, apply Scope Thesis without its stated preconditions, omit the evidence-ordered capstone delivery system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Scope Thesis for evidence-ordered capstone delivery system.

State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for evidence-ordered capstone delivery system.

The record names the assumption, applies Progress Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Progress Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Progress Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The review variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Progress Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Progress Evidence through the concrete evidence-ordered capstone delivery system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Progress Evidence, keep Progress Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.
